intro: Eriospora pilosa is a species of plant in the family Cucurbitaceae, commonly known as a type of hairy or woolly cucumber vine. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Central and South America, due to its purported health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections, wounds, and skin conditions.
